forms.Form 6: New voter registration for Indian citizens
forms.Form 7: Objection to inclusion or deletion from voter list
forms.Form 8: Correction/update of voter details (name, address, photo)
forms.Form 8A: Transposition of entry within same assembly constituency

process: Visit voters.eci.gov.in or use the Voter Helpline App
Click on 'New Registration' and fill Form 6
Upload required documents (photo, age proof, address proof)
Submit the application and note the reference ID
Track status using the reference ID on the portal
Receive Voter ID (EPIC card) by post or download e-EPIC from the portal
eligibility: Must be a citizen of India
Must be 18 years or older on the qualifying date (1st January of the registration year)
Must be a resident of the polling area where registration is sought
Must not be of unsound mind
Must not have been convicted of certain offences
documents_required: Age proof: Birth Certificate, Aadhaar Card, PAN Card, School Leaving Certificate, Passport
Address proof: Aadhaar Card, Utility bills (electricity/water), Bank passbook, Passport, Ration card
registration.epic_card: EPIC stands for Elector Photo Identity Card. It is your Voter ID card. You can download e-EPIC (digital version) from voters.eci.gov.in using your mobile number or EPIC number.
registration.aadhaar_linking: You can voluntarily link your Aadhaar with your Voter ID by filling Form 6B on the voter portal. This helps deduplicate voter rolls.
registration.overseas_voters: NRI voters can register using Form 6A. They vote at the Indian Embassy/Consulate in their country of residence.

election_types.Lok Sabha: General Election — 543 constituencies across India. Held every 5 years. Members are elected to the lower house of Parliament.
election_types.Rajya Sabha: Upper house of Parliament — 245 seats. Members are elected by State Legislative Assemblies, not directly by voters.
election_types.Vidhan Sabha: State Legislative Assembly elections. Each state has its own assembly. MLAs are directly elected.
election_types.Local Body Elections: Panchayat and Municipal elections conducted by State Election Commissions.

election_process.announcement: ECI announces election dates via a Press Conference. The Model Code of Conduct (MCC) comes into force immediately.
election_process.phases: Lok Sabha elections are held in multiple phases (typically 4-7 phases) across different states on different dates.
election_process.nomination: Candidates file nomination papers with the Returning Officer. Scrutiny of nominations is done, and candidates can withdraw within a specified period.
election_process.campaign: Campaigning is allowed after nomination. It ends 48 hours before polling (silent period).
election_process.polling_day: Voters go to their designated polling booth with valid ID proof. Vote using EVM.
election_process.counting: Counting of votes happens on the counting day at designated counting centers.
election_process.result: Winning candidate is declared by the Returning Officer.

voting.evm: EVM stands for Electronic Voting Machine. It consists of a Control Unit (with polling officer) and a Balloting Unit (with voter). Press the button next to your candidate's name and symbol.
voting.vvpat: VVPAT (Voter Verified Paper Audit Trail) is attached to EVMs. After pressing the button, a paper slip showing your candidate's name, symbol, and serial number is visible for 7 seconds before being cut and stored.
process: Bring your Voter ID (EPIC), Aadhaar, Passport, or any ECI-approved photo ID to the polling booth
Find your name in the voter list at the booth (the polling officer will verify)
Get the indelible ink mark on your left index finger
Proceed to the EVM and press the button next to your preferred candidate
Observe the VVPAT slip for confirmation
Exit the booth — your vote is cast!
valid_id_at_booth: Smart Card issued by RGI under NPR
voting.postal_ballot: Available for service voters (armed forces, police), persons with disabilities (PwD), voters above 85 years, and COVID-affected voters.
voting.proxy_voting: Service voters can appoint a proxy to vote on their behalf in their constituency.
voting.booth_slip: A booth slip with your serial number and booth details is distributed by political parties. It is NOT a mandatory document — you still need valid ID.

model_code_of_conduct.definition: MCC is a set of guidelines issued by ECI that political parties and candidates must follow during elections.
model_code_of_conduct.comes_into_force: Immediately upon announcement of election schedule
key_rules: No new government schemes can be announced by ruling party
Government machinery cannot be used for election campaigns
Candidates must not use religion or caste to seek votes
No hate speech or inflammatory statements
Campaign ends 48 hours before polling day
No alcohol distribution on polling day and day before

eligibility_details.nri_voting: NRIs can register as overseas electors using Form 6A and vote at their nearest Indian Embassy/Consulate.
pwd_facilities: Postal ballot option for PwD voters
PwD voters can call 1950 for help
eligibility_details.senior_citizen: Voters above 85 years of age can opt for postal ballot or home voting facility.
disqualification_grounds: Below 18 years of age on qualifying date
Declared of unsound mind by a competent court
Not a resident of the polling constituency
Disqualified under any law relating to corrupt practices or election offences

constituencies.reserved_seats: 84 seats reserved for SC (Scheduled Castes) and 47 for ST (Scheduled Tribes) in Lok Sabha
constituencies.delimitation: Constituencies are redrawn by the Delimitation Commission based on census data.
constituencies.find_constituency: Visit https://voters.eci.gov.in → 'Know Your Polling Station/Officer' to find your constituency by entering your name or EPIC number.

grievance.national_helpline: 1950 — Available Monday to Saturday, 10 AM to 5 PM
grievance.cvigil: cVIGIL app — Report MCC violations with photo or video. Download from Google Play or App Store. Reports are resolved within 100 minutes on average.
grievance.online_grievance: https://nationalhelpline.eci.gov.in — File complaints about voter registration, polling booth issues, etc.
what_to_report: Distribution of money or gifts to voters (bribery)
Violation of Model Code of Conduct
Removal or defacement of election symbols
grievance.state_ceo_tamil_nadu: Chief Electoral Officer, Tamil Nadu — https://www.elections.tn.gov.in
